mission.title: Install and Configure LocalAI
mission.description: LocalAI is a self-hosted, open-source alternative to proprietary AI models like OpenAI and Claude. It allows users to run various AI models locally without requiring GPU resources, making it suitable for consumer-grade hardware. Key trade-offs include the need for local infrastructure and potential limitations in model performance compared to cloud-based solutions.

steps.title: Install the LocalAI CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the LocalAI command-line interface, which is necessary for managing LocalAI services.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/go-skynet/LocalAI/releases/download/v3.12.1/localai-linux-amd64

chmod +x localai-linux-amd64

sudo mv localai-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/localai

```

> Download the LocalAI CLI binary.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kubernetes cluster using Minikube, which is suitable for local development and testing.

```bash

minikube start --kubernetes-version=v1.30.0 --cpus=2 --memory=4096

```

> Start Minikube with specified resources.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to interact with the Minikube cluster.

```bash

kubectl config use-context minikube

```

> Set the current context to Minikube.

steps.title: Deploy LocalAI on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys LocalAI on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add localai https://charts.localai.io

helm install localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai --create-namespace

```

> Add the LocalAI Helm repository.
> Install LocalAI using Helm.

steps.title: Verify LocalAI Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks if the LocalAI pods are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n localai

```

> List all pods in the LocalAI namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LocalAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes LocalAI from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources and data.

```bash

helm uninstall localai --namespace localai

kubectl delete namespace localai

```

> Uninstall LocalAI Helm release.
> Delete the LocalAI namespace and all its resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LocalAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step upgrades LocalAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade localai localai/localai --version 3.12.1 --namespace localai

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

```

> Cordon and drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade LocalAI to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified image may not exist or there may be network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure you have network access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LocalAI pod may be failing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n localai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server may be overloaded or unreachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of your Kubernetes cluster with 'kubectl cluster-info'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The node may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the CPU and memory limits in your Helm values or Minikube configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI models and improved performance for existing models.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the LocalAI service running with the output: 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' followed by the LocalAI pod details.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the required tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
